ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general beef rib entry (FOODON_03311737); no specific code for beef ribs in black pepper sauce. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for beef ribs in black pepper sauce. A0CKP (Beef meat, cooked) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226934003 represents beef ribs generically; no specific code for the black pepper sauce preparation exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical dim sum serving of beef ribs in black pepper sauce is approximately 150 g, comprising several pieces of bone-in short rib braised in sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, short ribs, cooked, braised — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 13352)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA SR Legacy braised beef short ribs (NDB 13352 / FDC 174033). Sodium adjusted upward to reflect black pepper sauce preparation (soy sauce, oyster sauce), consistent with typical Chinese braised beef rib dishes. Sugar and carbohydrate values reflect sauce contribution. All adaptations are conservative and documented.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for beef ribs in black pepper sauce. GI estimated at 25 based on analogous high-protein, low-carbohydrate braised meat dishes from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). The dish is predominantly protein and fat with minimal carbohydrate from sauce (3.2 g/100g), consistent with a low GI classification. GL per 150 g serving = (25 × 3.2 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce and oyster sauce in the black pepper sauce preparation
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of beef ribs in black pepper sauce due to an estimated sodium content of approximately 520 mg per 100 g (780 mg per 150 g serving), which represents a substantial proportion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced-sauce or sauce-on-side preparations is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Beef ribs in black pepper sauce have a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 25 and a glycemic load of approximately 1.2 per serving, making them a suitable protein-rich option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions and paired with low-GI vegetables rather than white rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat and cholesterol content from bone-in beef short ribs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Beef ribs in black pepper sauce contain approximately 9.1 g saturated fat and 93 mg cholesterol per 100 g, which may contribute to LDL cholesterol elevation; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to one dim sum serving (approximately 150 g) and avoid frequent consumption, while also noting the high sodium content from the sauce.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Beef Ribs in Black Pepper Sauce (黑椒牛肋骨) is a popular Chinese dim sum and restaurant dish consisting of bone-in beef short ribs that are braised or steamed until tender in a rich, aromatic black pepper sauce. The sauce typically combines black pepper, soy sauce, oyster sauce, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil, producing a deeply savoury, mildly spicy, and umami-forward flavour profile. The meat is characteristically tender and falls off the bone, with a slightly gelatinous texture from the collagen in the ribs. In Singapore, this dish is commonly served at Cantonese dim sum restaurants and Chinese zi char establishments. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, high-fat dish with moderate saturated fat and significant sodium from the sauce. The carbohydrate content is low, giving it a low glycemic index suitable for blood sugar management. However, the sodium content (approximately 520 mg per 100 g) makes it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The dish provides meaningful amounts of zinc, iron, vitamin B12, and phosphorus from the beef. Clinically, it is best consumed in moderation by those with cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and cholesterol content, and sodium intake should be monitored by hypertensive individuals.

How many calories in dim sum beef ribs?

Black pepper beef ribs GI index

confidence_reason: No exact match for beef ribs in black pepper sauce was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy braised beef short ribs (FDC 174033) with sodium adjusted upward to reflect the sauce preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database framework for high-protein, low-carbohydrate braised meat dishes. Confidence is medium due to the adaptation rather than exact source match.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for braised beef short ribs. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source for this preparation. Sodium value of 520 mg/100g is an upward adaptation from the USDA base value (approximately 60 mg/100g for unseasoned braised ribs) to reflect the soy sauce and oyster sauce content typical of Chinese black pepper sauce preparations; this is a conservative estimate consistent with similar sauced Chinese beef dishes. GI of 25 is an analogous estimate for a high-protein, low-carbohydrate braised meat dish; no direct GI measurement for this specific preparation was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GL per 150 g serving calculated as (25 × 3.2 × 1.5) / 100 = 1.2. Halal status is marked false as beef ribs from Chinese dim sum restaurants in Singapore may not be certified halal; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. The dish is served at Loong Dim Sum - North Canal Road per the source field survey data record (GD002927).
